sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the company's business type and primary CTA.
Business summary must explain what the company manufactures in concrete terms without vague marketing claims.
Product-category-summary should appear before the final CTA.
Trust proof must rely on real company facts such as organisation details, established processes or visible contact information.
Sections should avoid repeating the same claim in multiple blocks.
Products intro must describe the sortiment or manufacturing focus clearly and factually.
Product-category-list items must be specific categories or offerings, not generic placeholders.
If pricing-overview is shown, it should present honest indicative information only when such data exists.
Inquiry CTA should follow the main product listing.
Production intro must explain the manufacturing operation in plain language.
Process overview should describe steps, capabilities or workflows without disclosing unsupported technical claims.
Quality-and-compliance must stay factual and mention only real standards, controls or internal routines if provided.
Contact CTA must invite business inquiries after the operational information.
About story must be specific to the company and avoid generic brand language.
Team should only render when actual people or roles are available.
Company facts should include concrete business information such as organisation details or location when supplied.
Contact info must contain real available contact methods and no placeholders.
Contact form should be positioned after or beside the primary contact details.
Map and opening hours should only render when verified address and schedule data exist.
